agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Walk across the floor and wait the archway. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my current surroundings.

message.content: I'll start by observing my current surroundings.

messages.content: I can see a room with a pool table on a rug, with large windows and what appears to be an archway on the left side. Let me look around to get a full picture of the space.

message.content: I can see a room with a pool table on a rug, with large windows and what appears to be an archway on the left side. Let me look around to get a full picture of the space.

messages.content: I can see the current room has a pool table, and behind me there's a beautiful archway leading to another room with wood paneling. This looks like the archway I need to walk to. Let me turn around and head toward it.

message.content: I can see the current room has a pool table, and behind me there's a beautiful archway leading to another room with wood paneling. This looks like the archway I need to walk to. Let me turn around and head toward it.

messages.content: Now I can see the archway clearly ahead. Let me walk forward across the floor toward it.

message.content: Now I can see the archway clearly ahead. Let me walk forward across the floor toward it.

messages.content: I'm getting closer to the archway. Let me continue moving toward it.

message.content: I'm getting closer to the archway. Let me continue moving toward it.

messages.content: I'm now close to the archway - about 2 meters away. This looks like the archway I need to stop at. Let me move a bit closer to be right at it.

message.content: I'm now close to the archway - about 2 meters away. This looks like the archway I need to stop at. Let me move a bit closer to be right at it.

messages.content: I'm now positioned right at the archway, having walked across the floor from the pool table room. This matches the instruction "walk across the floor and wait at the archway." Let me stop here.

message.content: I'm now positioned right at the archway, having walked across the floor from the pool table room. This matches the instruction "walk across the floor and wait at the archway." Let me stop here.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 89 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 11, "tool_calls_remaining": 89}

info.message: STOP not executed (first request). You still have 89 tool calls — verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.

messages.content: Let me verify my position with a full look around before confirming.

messages.content: I can see behind me is a large, prominent archway with a stone/wood arch design leading back to the pool table room. This is likely the main archway referenced in the instruction. Let me turn around and position myself right at that archway.

message.content: I can see behind me is a large, prominent archway with a stone/wood arch design leading back to the pool table room. This is likely the main archway referenced in the instruction. Let me turn around and position myself right at that archway.

messages.content: This is the grand archway I walked through from the pool table room. Let me move closer to be right at the archway opening.

message.content: This is the grand archway I walked through from the pool table room. Let me move closer to be right at the archway opening.

messages.content: I'm now right at the archway opening, with the glass doors and pool table room visible beyond. This is a clear archway position. Let me confirm this is a good stopping point and issue STOP.
